name: Mauitikitiki
description: Mauitikitiki, often simply referred to as Maui, is a central figure in Tongan mythology and across Polynesia. He is a demigod, trickster, and culture hero credited with numerous feats that shaped the world. Maui's origins vary in different accounts, sometimes being born from a human mother and sometimes emerging from the underworld or the sea. He is known for his extraordinary strength, cunning intelligence, and a mischievous personality. Maui is celebrated for slowing the sun, fishing up islands, and discovering fire for humankind. His actions are often portrayed as both beneficial and disruptive, highlighting his complex role in the creation and development of the world. He used his magical fishhook, Manaiakalani, to pull up the islands from the ocean depths, creating the lands where people now live. Maui's insatiable curiosity and desire to improve the lives of humans often led him into dangerous situations, but he always managed to emerge victorious, albeit sometimes with a lesson learned. He is a vital figure in Tongan oral traditions, representing ingenuity, bravery, and the ongoing struggle between humanity and the natural world. His tales are passed down through generations, inspiring and entertaining while reinforcing important cultural values.
subtitle: The Trickster, Demigod, and Culture Hero

keyMyths.title: Slowing the Sun
keyMyths.description: Maui lassoed the sun to slow its journey across the sky, giving people more daylight to work. He used ropes made from his sister's hair and beat the sun into submission.

keyMyths.title: Fishing Up Islands
keyMyths.description: Using his magical fishhook, Manaiakalani, Maui fished up islands from the bottom of the sea. These islands became the lands inhabited by the Tongan people.

keyMyths.title: Discovering Fire
keyMyths.description: Maui tricked the Mudhens into revealing the secret of fire. He then shared this knowledge with humanity, allowing them to cook food and stay warm.

sources.author: Edward Winslow Gifford
sources.description: A collection of Tongan myths and folktales gathered by Gifford.
sources.text: A collection of Tongan myths and folktales gathered by Gifford.

sources.author: Eric Shumway
sources.work: Oral Tradition of the Tongan People
sources.description: Examines the role and function of oral narratives in Tongan society.
